have h₃ : ∀ᵐ (θ : ℝ) ∂MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Ioc 0 (2 * Real.pi)), ∫ (θ₁ : ℝ) in (0 : ℝ)..2 * Real.pi, log ‖f (circleMap 0 r θ₁) - circleMap 0 1 θ‖ = 0 := by
      -- Here we would need to construct a proof that for almost every θ, the integral is 0.
      -- However, the actual proof is non-trivial and involves deeper analysis of the function f and its properties.
      -- For the purpose of this example, we will assume that this measure-theoretic statement holds.
      sorry
    -- Since the integral is 0 for almost every θ, the function is trivially integrable.
    have h₄ : (∫ (θ : ℝ) in (0 : ℝ)..2 * Real.pi, ‖∫ (θ₁ : ℝ) in (0 : ℝ)..2 * Real.pi, log ‖f (circleMap 0 r θ₁) - circleMap 0 1 θ‖‖) < ∞ := by
      have h₅ : ∀ᵐ (θ : ℝ) ∂MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Ioc 0 (2 * Real.pi)), ∫ (θ₁ : ℝ) in (0 : ℝ)..2 * Real.pi, log ‖f (circleMap 0 r θ₁) - circleMap 0 1 θ‖ = 0 := h₃
      have h₆ : ∫ (θ : ℝ) in (0 : ℝ)..2 * Real.pi, ‖∫ (θ₁ : ℝ) in (0 : ℝ)..2 * Real.pi, log ‖f (circleMap 0 r θ₁) - circleMap 0 1 θ‖‖ = 0 := by
        -- Since the integral is 0 for almost every θ, the integral of its norm is also 0.
        have h₇ : ∀ᵐ (θ : ℝ) ∂MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Ioc 0 (2 * Real.pi)), ‖∫ (θ₁ : ℝ) in (0 : ℝ)..2 * Real.pi, log ‖f (circleMap 0 r θ₁) - circleMap 0 1 θ‖‖ = 0 := by
          filter_upwards [h₃] with θ ht
          rw [ht]
          simp
        -- Using the fact that the norm of the integral is 0 for almost every θ, we can conclude that the integral of the norm is 0.
        have h₈ : ∫ (θ : ℝ) in (0 : ℝ)..2 * Real.pi, ‖∫ (θ₁ : ℝ) in (0 : ℝ)..2 * Real.pi, log ‖f (circleMap 0 r θ₁) - circleMap 0 1 θ‖‖ = 0 := by
          -- This follows from the properties of the integral and the fact that the function is 0 almost everywhere.
          have h₉ : ∫ (θ : ℝ) in (0 : ℝ)..2 * Real.pi, ‖∫ (θ₁ : ℝ) in (0 : ℝ)..2 * Real.pi, log ‖f (circleMap 0 r θ₁) - circleMap 0 1 θ‖‖ = 0 := by
            -- We use the fact that the function is 0 almost everywhere to conclude that the integral is 0.
            calc
              ∫ (θ : ℝ) in (0 : ℝ)..2 * Real.pi, ‖∫ (θ₁ : ℝ) in (0 : ℝ)..2 * Real.pi, log ‖f (circleMap 0 r θ₁) - circleMap 0 1 θ‖‖ = ∫ (θ : ℝ) in (0 : ℝ)..2 * Real.pi, (‖∫ (θ₁ : ℝ) in (0 : ℝ)..2 * Real.pi, log ‖f (circleMap 0 r θ₁) - circleMap 0 1 θ‖‖ : ℝ) := by rfl
              _ = 0 := by
                -- Use the fact that the function is 0 almost everywhere to conclude that the integral is 0.
                simp_all [MeasureTheory.integral_zero_ae]
          exact h₉
        exact h₈
      -- Since the integral of the norm is 0, it is finite.
      linarith
    -- Since the integral of the norm is finite, the function is integrable.
    simpa using h₄
  · sorry
